ocr.text: In the context of the rapidly evolving oil and gas sector within the Asia-Pacific region, well completion efficiency emerges as a
critical metric in optimizing operational performance and enhancing profitability. As companies like ExxonMobil strive to adapt
to increasing market demands and environmental considerations, it is essential to evaluate key performance indicators that
reflect operational effectiveness. For instance, the average completion time for wells targeted in this report is represented by a
notable metric of 25 Days, highlighting ongoing efforts to streamline processes.
WellCount  CompletedWells  ProductionRate  DrillingTime  CompletionCost  TotalVolume  GasVolume  OilVolume  WaterVolume
Region  WellType  EfficiencyRate  CostperWell  DaystoComplete  ProductionUptime  RevenueperWell  Headcount  ProjectDuration
Asia-Pacific  Onshore  Offshore  Total  MonthlyAverage  Gas(Mcf)  Oil(bbl)  Water(bbl)  Total(bbl)
2025  Total  Average  Peak  Minimum  Maximum  NetRevenue  CostRecovery  TotalAssets  Investment
Furthermore, the data reveals that the average production rate of these wells is projected to reach 80,000 barrels per day by
2025, symbolizing a significant milestone in operational efficiency. Notably, the report indicates that 90% of wells completed
within the specified timeframe achieved an impressive efficiency rate of 80%. Additionally, insights gathered from the analysis
show that the cost associated with each well completion is estimated at 40,000 KRW, which underscores the financial
implications of efficiency in the overall project lifecycle. Given the challenging landscape, where competition is fierce, and
regulatory pressures are mounting, organizations can greatly benefit from understanding these metrics. In particular, the average
efficiency for well completions is set at 50%, with companies aiming to enhance this rate through innovative technologies and
improved practices, ensuring they remain competitive in a dynamic market environment.
